layers.name: WASM Cores
layers.description: Foundational WebAssembly crates that expose RuVector core primitives (HNSW, CNN, DAG, graph, math, learning) to browser hosts.

layers.name: WASM Attention & Mincut Family
layers.description: WASM crates implementing attention mechanisms, mincut partitioning, gated transformers, and hyperbolic HNSW for advanced retrieval.

layers.name: Node Bindings
layers.description: Node.js / CLI bindings that wrap RuVector subsystems (attention, GNN, graph, DiskANN, mincut) for server-side JavaScript consumers.

layers.name: Specialized WASM
layers.description: Domain-specialized WASM and companion native crates: GNN, RaBitQ quantization, domain expansion, economy/exotic encoders, and sparsifiers.

layers.name: Routing
layers.description: Router stack — core routing engine plus CLI, FFI, and WASM surfaces for embedding RuVector routing in heterogeneous hosts.

layers.name: Search Infrastructure
layers.description: Native search and supporting infrastructure: DiskANN, hyperbolic HNSW, benchmarking, server, profiling, metrics, collections, CRV, dither, and filter utilities.

layers.name: Thermal & Profiling
layers.description: Thermal-aware runtime crates and profiling utilities (ruos-thermal, thermorust, profiling) for hardware-conscious scheduling.

tour.title: Satellite Cluster Overview
tour.description: Slice 10f groups RuVector's 46 satellite crates — small, focused packages that wrap the core engine for specific deployment targets (browser WASM, Node.js, FFI, CLI) and specialized domains (attention, mincut, RaBitQ, thermal). Start here with the headline READMEs of the WASM core and routing crates to see how a satellite is structured: a thin Cargo.toml, a lib.rs surface, and a published pkg/README documenting the JS/host-side API.

tour.title: WASM Cores — Primitive Bindings
tour.description: The WASM cores expose RuVector's foundational primitives to browser hosts: micro-hnsw-wasm packages the standalone HNSW index, ruvector-cnn-wasm exports a contrastive embedder with InfoNCE/Triplet losses and SIMD ops, and ruvector-dag-wasm surfaces the DAG engine. Each crate uses wasm-bindgen to project Rust structs (MicroHnsw, WasmCnnEmbedder) into JavaScript classes with explicit init() and per-op methods.

tour.title: WASM Attention & Mincut Family
tour.description: Building on the WASM cores, this family layers retrieval-quality features: ruvector-attention-wasm and the larger ruvector-attention-unified-wasm bundle attention scoring, ruvector-mincut-wasm and ruvector-mincut-gated-transformer-wasm offer mincut-based partitioning of candidate sets, and ruvector-hyperbolic-hnsw-wasm provides the curved-space HNSW variant. These crates compose with the cores from Step 2 to deliver advanced retrieval entirely in-browser.

tour.title: Node.js Bindings — Server-Side Bridge
tour.description: Where Steps 2-3 target the browser, this layer crosses into Node.js via napi-rs/neon. Each *-node crate (attention, gnn, graph, diskann, mincut) is a thin adapter exposing native-speed RuVector subsystems to server-side JS, while ruvector-attention-cli demonstrates the same primitives behind a standalone command-line. The pattern mirrors the WASM crates but trades portability for native performance.

tour.title: Specialized WASM — Domain Encoders
tour.description: These crates package domain-specific encoders and compression techniques: ruvector-gnn-wasm runs graph neural nets in-browser, ruvector-rabitq-wasm implements RaBitQ binary quantization, ruvector-domain-expansion(-wasm) widens vectors with learned projections, ruvector-economy-wasm and ruvector-exotic-wasm provide alternate encoding schemes, and ruvector-sparsifier-wasm prunes dense embeddings. Each pairs a native crate (the algorithm) with a -wasm crate (the binding).

tour.title: Routing Stack — Core, CLI, FFI, WASM
tour.description: The router is RuVector's embeddable vector-routing engine, designed to drop into heterogeneous hosts. ruvector-router-core holds the index, distance kernels (euclidean/cosine/dot/manhattan), and error model; ruvector-router-cli wraps it for shell use; ruvector-router-ffi exposes a C ABI for non-Rust callers; ruvector-router-wasm publishes the same engine as a browser module. Notice how one core crate fans out to three transport-specific surfaces.

tour.title: Search Infrastructure — Native Engines
tour.description: Native, server-side search and supporting plumbing: ruvector-diskann is the on-disk ANN index, ruvector-hyperbolic-hnsw is its curved-space sibling, ruvector-server hosts them over HTTP, ruvector-bench runs reproducible benchmarks, and ruvector-profiler/ruvector-metrics expose observability hooks. ruvector-collections, ruvector-crv, ruvector-dither, and ruvector-filter supply the data structures and post-processing utilities that wrap around the indexes.

tour.title: Thermal & Profiling — Hardware-Aware Runtime
tour.description: The capstone layer makes RuVector hardware-conscious. ruos-thermal reads CPU temperatures, derives ClockProfiles, and recommends target frequencies so search workloads back off before thermal throttling; thermorust offers a lower-level temperature crate; the profiling utility provides a unified surface for the metrics emitted by Step 7's engines. Together they let operators close the loop between query load, silicon heat, and routing decisions made by the Step 6 router.
